free_text: Pygmy dipole strength in 86Kr and systematics of N =
50 isotones
Measurement performed at ELBE
facility in Rossendorf
Assymetry measured at HIgS facility
High-pressure gas target in
spherical container made of stainless steel with an
inner diameter of 20mm and a wall thickness of 0.5mm
containing 1012.6 mg of enriched 86Kr.Target was shaped
into a disk with diameter of 20 mm.
11-B enriched to 99.5% was
added to determine cross sections relative  to 11B(g,n)
Bremsstrahlung was produced using electron
beams with kinetic energy of 7.9 and 11.2 MeV. The
average currents were about 550 mu-A in the
measurement at 7.9 MeV and about 720 mu-A in the
measurement at 11.2 MeV. The electron beams hit a
niobium foil of 7 mu-m thickness acting as a radiator.
Scattered photons were measured with four high-
purity germanium (HPGe) detectors that have an
efficiency of 100% relative to a NaI detector of 7.6
cm in diameter and 7.6 cm in length. All HPGe
detectors were surrounded by escape-suppression
shields made of bismuth germanate BGO scintillation
detectors of 3 cm in thickness. Two HPGe detectors
were placed vertically at 90deg relative to the
photon-beam direction and a distance of 28 cm from
the target. The other two HPGe detectors were
positioned in a horizontal plane at 127deg to the
beam and at a distance of 32 cm from the target.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.

azimuthal asymmetry, respectively, of the ground-state
transition.

is an average over the values obtained at 90deg and
127deg.Below an excitation energy of 7.0 MeV the value
was deduced from the measurement at 7.9 MeV electron
energy, otherwise it is from the measurement
at 11.2 MeV
